title: hugonia neocaledonica
intro: Hugonia neocaledonica, also known as the 'Mamala' or 'Neocaledonian Hugonia', is a species of flowering plant in the family Linaceae. This plant is native to New Caledonia and is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Hugonia neocaledonica include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic and antispasmodic. Herbal preparations of Hugonia neocaledonica can be made by infusing the leaves and roots in hot water or by extracting the plant's active compounds using solvents, and it is often used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
